author: @antonnym214
text: Kindness like that reminds me of something I heard a long time ago that a radio psychologist said. The host asked the doctor what someone should do if they felt they were on the verge of a nervous breakdown. The doctor replied that if you are feeling that way, you should find someone who needs help and help them.  What great advice! In helping others we are also becoming better people ourselves. Well done Syd!

author: @nicholascup126
text:
Hello. Love watching your vid's.
I keep thinking about your turbine and how only 1 section at a time is actually catching and is using the wind, and of course that would be the blades that are catching the wind. While the other blades are not opposing the wind as much, it would be nice if the to could be made to help, or at least use one other section.
So I was thinking of a scoop that would redirect air to more blades, that might be guided by a vain type system so it to always catches the wind.
Just a thought, and I know would be a lot of work. But at least with the thought out there, it won't dry up and die in just my head.

Again, thank you for you video's, and your thoughts that I think more people should live by. And I really like your closing phrasing. Keep up the great work, and i wish you nothing but the best.

Nick

author: @goldmagnet9013
text: Rob, have you considered the resistance of the microwave coil windings ? They are designed to produce very high voltages, and tend to have high internal resistance (25 ~ 100 ohms), which makes them useless for electricity production as with any substantial current power is lost in the windings to heat..  Have a look at Hugh Piggott's papers on coil design in windmill stators. You could dismantle them and use the wire, but using them as you intend will cripple power production.

replies.author: @goldmagnet9013
replies.text: @fredcaveman3892 You misunderstand, electrical resistance not magnetic.

replies.author: @goldmagnet9013
replies.text: Hi Rob, I apologise, I rewatched part 5 where you are talking on the coils and you say you are most likely going to use the primary running at high voltage. Running at high voltage solves the heat issue in the coil but is no good for charging so are you going to make a controller to step this down to charging voltage for a battery ?

author: @alext8828
text: Yeah, Syd is very kind.  Not only is he a wellwisher, but he got off his ass about it.  I wish everyone well but try and get me off my ass.  Good for him and you.  Glad you got the items from him.  More glad that you're not giving up on your (our) project.  I want to see you win the I'm Better Than Niagara Falls Award which doesn't really exist, but should.  We're all still hanging in there, so don't get discouraged.
